title: Snapped Phish-ing Line (THM)

headings.text: 1- Who is the individual who received an email attachment containing a PDF?
2- What email address was used by the adversary to send the phishing emails?
3- What is the redirection URL to the phishing page for the individual Zoe Duncan? (defanged format)
4- What is the URL to the .zip archive of the phishing kit? (defanged format)
5- What is the SHA256 hash of the phishing kit archive?
6- When was the phishing kit archive first submitted? (format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC)
7- When was the SSL certificate the phishing domain used to host the phishing kit archive first logged? (format: YYYY-MM-DD)
8- What was the email address of the user who submitted their password twice?
9- What was the email address used by the adversary to collect compromised credentials?
10- The adversary used other email addresses in the obtained phishing kit. What is the email address that ends in "@gmail.com"?

overview: As an IT department personnel of SwiftSpend Financial, one of your responsibilities is to support your fellow employees with their technical concerns. While everything seemed ordinary and mundane, this gradually changed when several employees from various departments started reporting an unusual email they had received. study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: 1- Who is the individual who received an email attachment containing a PDF?
2- What email address was used by the adversary to send the phishing emails?
3- What is the redirection URL to the phishing page for the individual Zoe Duncan? (defanged format)
4- What is the URL to the .zip archive of the phishing kit? (defanged format)
5- What is the SHA256 hash of the phishing kit archive?
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
